steps.title: Track the passage structure
steps.content: The text first explains that both wild and lab studies have advantages: normal behavior is easier to presume in the wild, while factors are easier to control in the lab. It then adds that conflicting results show one or both methods may have missed a relevant factor.

steps.title: State the balanced main idea
steps.content: Choice A ranks wild studies above lab studies, which the text does not do. Choice B says conflicts are likely and unresolvable, going beyond the text. Choice D says both studies must be flawed, but the text says one or both. The balanced point is that both approaches work but have drawbacks. The answer is C.
